body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35350", "cvss": {"score": 6.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-281", "name": "Improper Preservation of Permissions"}], "description": "The cp utility in uutils coreutils fails to properly handle setuid and setgid bits when ownership preservation fails. When copying with the -p (preserve) flag, the utility applies the source mode bits even if the chown operation is unsuccessful. This can result in a user-owned copy retaining original privileged bits, creating unexpected privileged executables that violate local security policies. This differs from GNU cp, which clears these bits when ownership cannot be preserved.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00125, "percentile": 0.02555}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-x2wv-9p67-mh9w", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-29T23:18:25Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x2wv-9p67-mh9w", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-x2wv-9p67-mh9w"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35350"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-22T17:16:37Z", "published_at": "2026-04-22T18:31:45Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35350", "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils/issues/9750", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-x2wv-9p67-mh9w"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils", "summary": "uutils coreutils doesn't properly handle setuid and setgid bits when ownership preservation fails", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T23:18:26Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-x2wv-9p67-mh9w", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "coreutils"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.8.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: uutils coreutils doesn't properly handle setuid and setgid bits when ownership preservation fails
